claim: GitHub Actions defines workflow_dispatch (manual UI/CLI/API) and repository_dispatch (external API) as the two remote-trigger primitives.
quote: You can use the GitHub API to trigger a webhook event called repository_dispatch when you want to trigger a workflow for activity that happens outside of GitHub.

claim: workflow_dispatch supports up to 10 typed inputs and can be fired from the UI, REST API, or gh CLI; the workflow file must already be on the chosen branch.
quote: You can configure custom-defined input properties, default input values, and required inputs for the event directly in your workflow.

claim: gh workflow run posts to /actions/workflows/{id}/dispatches; --ref selects the branch/tag the workflow runs from and -f sets inputs.
quote: Run a given workflow file. The given workflow file must support an on.workflow_dispatch trigger.

claim: peter-evans/repository-dispatch is the standard wrapper for firing a repository_dispatch event from inside another workflow or external system.
quote: A GitHub action to create a repository dispatch event.

claim: repository_dispatch fires only against the default branch — the workflow file must live on the default branch and ref/branch context cannot be chosen by the caller.
quote: repository_dispatch only triggers on the master branch, and it is not possible to specify a branch.

claim: Anything pushed by GITHUB_TOKEN deliberately does NOT trigger downstream workflows, to prevent recursive runs — including a PR that the action just opened.
quote: When you use the repository's GITHUB_TOKEN to perform tasks, events triggered by the GITHUB_TOKEN will not create a new workflow run.

claim: peter-evans/create-pull-request is the de-facto action to take whatever is in the workspace, branch it, commit, and open or update a PR; current release v8 (Apr 2026).
quote: A GitHub action to create a pull request for changes to your repository in the actions workspace.

claim: If you want PRs created by the action to trigger an on:push or on:pull_request workflow, you cannot use GITHUB_TOKEN — you must use a PAT with workflow scope or a GitHub App token.
quote: If you want pull requests created by this action to trigger an on: push or on: pull_request workflow then you cannot use the default GITHUB_TOKEN.

claim: slash-command-dispatch reads issue/PR comments for /commands and re-emits them as repository_dispatch events so heavy work runs in a separate workflow.
quote: Checks the first line of comments for slash commands. When a valid command is found it creates a repository dispatch event.

claim: ChatOps pattern: dispatching from a thin issue_comment handler keeps the comment-event queue moving and lets command workflows run in parallel.
quote: Dispatching commands to be processed elsewhere keeps the workflow queue moving quickly and essentially enables parallel processing of workflows.

claim: anthropics/claude-code-action runs in issue_comment / issues / PR contexts, detects @claude mentions or assignment, branches, edits, runs tests and opens a PR.
quote: A general purpose Claude Code action for GitHub PRs and issues.

claim: Claude Code GitHub Actions activates on @claude mentions in issues, issue comments, PR review comments, or by issue assignment.
quote: Mention @claude in any PR or issue and watch as Claude analyzes, plans, and executes.

claim: GitHub Copilot coding agent runs in an ephemeral GitHub Actions environment, branches, commits, pushes, and opens a draft PR — triggered by issue assignment, the agents panel, @copilot in a PR comment, or an explicit prompt.
quote: Powered by GitHub Actions, the coding agent operates in its own ephemeral development environment.

claim: Copilot coding agent reached general availability for paid Copilot subscribers in 2026.
quote: Copilot coding agent, our asynchronous, autonomous developer agent, is now generally available for all paid Copilot subscribers.

claim: OpenAI Codex is a cloud-and-terminal coding agent with a hosted variant (Codex Cloud) that opens PRs against connected GitHub repos.
quote: Lightweight coding agent that runs in your terminal.

claim: Tagging @codex on a GitHub issue or PR spins up a hosted task that produces a pull request from Codex Cloud.
quote: Tag @codex on issues and pull requests to spin up tasks and propose changes directly from GitHub.

claim: Devin is a fully-hosted autonomous engineer assigned tasks from Slack, Linear, Jira or the web app; it clones, branches, runs tests and opens a PR on GitHub.
quote: Devin is the world's first AI software engineer.

claim: Devin's 2026 release notes show MCP-auth status surfacing in Slack and a new PR Resuming feature for taking over existing PRs.
quote: PR Resuming, where Devin can now take over and work on existing pull requests that weren't created in the current session.

claim: Cursor's GitHub integration lets you @cursor in any PR or issue to fire a background agent that branches, edits and pushes.
quote: Trigger an agent to read context, implement fixes, and push commits by commenting @cursor [prompt] on any PR or issue.

claim: Cursor agents can be triggered by external events: Slack message, new Linear issue, merged GitHub PR, or PagerDuty incident.
quote: Agents can run on schedules or be triggered by events like a sent Slack message, a newly created Linear issue, a merged GitHub PR.

claim: Aider is an open-source CLI pair-programmer; one of its common deployments is as a GitHub Action triggered by a label.
quote: aider is AI pair programming in your terminal.

claim: aider-github-action runs Aider against an issue, commits to a fresh branch, and pushes — typically wired to fire when an 'aider' label is added.
quote: Run Aider AI assistant in your Github Workflows!

claim: Renovate by Mend is a multi-platform dependency-update bot that opens grouped PRs across GitHub, GitLab, Bitbucket, Azure DevOps, Forgejo, Gitea.
quote: Cross-platform Dependency Automation by Mend.io.

claim: Renovate vs Dependabot: Renovate groups monorepo updates into a single PR by default; Dependabot opens one PR per dependency unless explicitly grouped.
quote: Built-in preset that upgrades common monorepo packages in a single PR.

claim: GitLab's equivalent of repository_dispatch is the trigger token: POST to /projects/:id/trigger/pipeline with a token and ref to start a pipeline on any branch.
quote: An API call to the pipeline triggers API endpoint can trigger a pipeline for a specific branch or tag.

claim: GitLab's Merge Requests API supports POST to create an MR — the analogue of peter-evans/create-pull-request when the trigger is external.
quote: Creates a new merge request.
